mnemonics: BFS: Breadth-First Search uses a Queue (First In, First Out)
DFS: Depth-First Search uses a Stack (Last In, First Out)
Uniform Cost Search (UCS): Always expands the least cost node first, like Dijkstra's algorithm
keyPoints: Uninformed search strategies do not use domain-specific knowledge. They are also called blind search.
BFS is complete and optimal if all step costs are identical.
DFS is not complete (may get stuck in loops) and not optimal.
UCS is complete and optimal if step costs are non-negative.
commonMistakes: Confusing BFS (queue) with DFS (stack) data structures.
Forgetting that DFS can get stuck in infinite loops without cycle checking.
Assuming UCS is the same as BFS; UCS uses path cost, not depth.
